---
title: Report
---

# Report

## Motivation behind the project

Hook (1–2 sentences):

AI research has exploded in the past few years—but where is it actually going?

### Goal:

Track how major AI/LLM topics evolved from 2019–2026

### Key Questions:

Which AI topics grew the fastest from 2019 to 2026?
Which topics had the highest publication volume each year?
Which topics had the highest citation impact?
How concentrated or diverse was AI research across topics over time?
Did publication volume in selected topics increase after landmark papers?

## Methodology

### Data Source

We searched through several different database options for aquiring data about research papers. After exploring options such as OpenAlex and arXiv, we finally settled on SemanticScholar because their API easily provided sufficient data to analyze.

### Search Terms
We used the following search terms when curating our dataset. 

- llm
- CoT
- reasoning
- chain of thought
- rag
- grounding
- external memory
- multimodal
- image generation
- rlhf
- lora
- qlora
- quantization
- efficient fine-tuning
- long context and efficient
- sparse attention
- flash attention
- hallucination
- efficient inference

### Dataset

The resulting dataset had ___ distinct entries.
Columns included the following:
- title
- authors
- year
- citations
- abstract
- paperId
- influential_citations	

### Embedding

### Analysis

### Landmark Papers

(Keep to 3–4 max)

GPT-3 (2020) → scaling + few-shot
RAG (2020) → retrieval-based models
InstructGPT (2022) → alignment
Chain-of-Thought (2022) → reasoning

Goal:

See if these triggered growth in related topics

## Insights

(This is your main section—very scannable)

### Overall Growth of AI Research
Total papers increased from [X] → [Y]
Growth accelerated around [year]

Key takeaway:

[1 sentence insight]

### Fastest Growing Topics
#1: [Topic] → +[X%]
#2: [Topic]
#3: [Topic]

Observation:

“Growth is concentrated in…”
### Most Popular Topics by Volume
2019: [Topic]
2022: [Topic]
2026: [Topic]

Trend:

“We see a shift from ___ → ___”
### Highest Impact Topics (Citations)
[Topic] has highest avg citations
[Topic] has high volume but lower impact

Interesting insight:

“Popular ≠ impactful because…”
### How Diverse Is AI Research?
Early years: concentrated in [few topics]
Later years: more spread out / more specialized

Interpretation:

“The field is becoming more [specialized/diverse]”
### Did Landmark Papers Change Trends?

Example 1: GPT-3 (2020)

Before: [trend]
After: [trend]
“Clear increase in LLM-related work”

Example 2: Chain-of-Thought (2022)

spike in reasoning papers

(Only include strong effects—skip weak ones)

## Takeaways

### Key Insights

AI research is shifting toward [X]
Some topics grow fast but don’t dominate volume
Breakthrough papers can trigger rapid follow-on work

### Limitations

Topic classification is imperfect
arXiv bias (not all papers included)
Citations lag behind recent work

### Conclusion

AI research is not just growing—it’s shifting direction
Recent years show a move toward:
[example: LLMs, reasoning, efficiency]

Closing line (keep it punchy):

“Understanding these trends helps us see not just where AI is—but where it’s going next.”
